name: From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route
name_original: De Murtosa a Ovar — percurso circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Murtosa are primarily shaped by the Ria de Aveiro, a shallow coastal lagoon that defines much of the region's landscape. The terrain is generally flat, offering accessible rides, with some routes providing slight variations in elevation. This area features an extensive network of cycling paths that traverse canals, branches, and piers of the Ria, as well as coastal sections. Best road cycling routes around Murtosa The most popular road cycling route is From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route offers scenic views along the Ria de Aveiro. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Estarreja to Ponte do poço de Santiago — circular route passing on the Ecopista do Vouga, a difficult 41.9 miles (67.4 km) path. This route features varied terrain and includes sections along the Ecopista do Vouga. Local road cyclists also love the Margres Aveiro Spring Classic – Carcavelos Bridge loop from Estarreja, a 94.7 miles (152.4 km) trail leading through the wider Aveiro region, often completed in about 6 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Murtosa is defined by the Ria de Aveiro's lagoon landscapes, coastal paths, and generally flat terrain. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Murtosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 2,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Murtosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Murtosa?
A: Murtosa offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 50 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy scenic rides to more challenging long-distance tours.

Q: What is the typical terrain and difficulty level for road cycling in Murtosa?
A: The terrain in Murtosa is generally flat, making it accessible for many cyclists. However, some routes do offer slight variations in elevation for those seeking a bit more challenge. On komoot, you'll find 11 easy routes, 32 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the road cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: Road cycling in Murtosa is defined by the stunning landscapes of the Ria de Aveiro, a shallow coastal lagoon. You'll encounter diverse scenery including canals, branches, and piers of the Ria. Notable sights include the traditional &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3893934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moliceiro Boats on the Canals of Aveiro&lt;/a&gt;, sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;EV1 on the Ria de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt;, and coastal areas like Torreira Beach. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6026676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aveiro Friendship Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5367241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; are also points of interest in the wider region.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: Yes, Murtosa offers several easy, generally flat routes that are suitable for families. These routes often run along the Ria de Aveiro, providing scenic and safe environments away from heavy traffic. The "Murtosa Ciclável" project actively promotes cycling for urban mobility and discovering local heritage, which includes accessible paths.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Murtosa?
A: The road cycling routes in Murtosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the tranquil beauty of the Ria de Aveiro, the well-maintained cycling infrastructure, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities, from relaxed coastal rides to more extensive tours.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Murtosa?
A: Absolutely. Many of the road cycling routes in Murtosa are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384062230/from-murtosa-to-ovar-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Murtosa to Ovar — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views along the Ria de Aveiro.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Murtosa and the wider Aveiro region offer longer and more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384002830/from-estarreja-to-ponte-do-poco-de-santiago-circular-route-passing-on-the-ecopista-do-vouga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Estarreja to Ponte do poço de Santiago — circular route passing on the Ecopista do Vouga&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 67.4 km path with varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6929148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Margres Aveiro Spring Classic – Carcavelos Bridge loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 152.4 km trail.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Murtosa?
A: Murtosa is suitable for road cycling for much of the year, but August is noted as a particularly popular month. The region's mild climate generally allows for pleasant riding conditions, especially during spring and autumn. Summer offers warm weather, ideal for coastal rides, while winter can be cooler but still manageable.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the coastal areas or beaches?
A: Yes, Murtosa's unique geography includes coastal and lagoon paths. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32256579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreira Marina – EV1 on the Ria de Aveiro loop from Torreira&lt;/a&gt; offer views of the Ria de Aveiro and access to coastal beaches such as Monte Branco Beach and Torreira Beach, providing opportunities for scenic rides along the shore.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other activities in Murtosa?
A: Absolutely. The Ria de Aveiro, which is central to many cycling routes, is also an ideal spot for birdwatching and water activities like rowing, sailing, canoeing, and stand-up paddleboarding. You can also explore cultural points of interest such as the Custódio Prato House-Museum or the COMUR-Museu Municipal, which celebrates the eel canning industry, along or near your cycling routes.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty routes that offer a good balance of challenge and scenery?
A: For those looking for a balanced ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32192229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dune Forest of Ovar – Ponte da Varela loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 102.9 km route. It provides a good distance and varied scenery without the extreme elevation gains of more difficult trails, allowing you to enjoy the landscapes at a comfortable pace.
tips: Inaugurated on August 16, 1981, the monument in honor of the Murtosa emigrants, the work of José João Brito, was celebrated with an open mass and a parade by local associations, highlighting the presence of the flags of countries relevant to the Murtoseira emigration. The statue was unveiled by João Campos, Murtosa's oldest emigrant.

Estarreja is known for the Ria de Aveiro, the annual Carnival and Foral do Antuã of D. Manuel I and for its unique urban art.

At the ESTAU Festival, started by the artist Bordallo II, artists from all over the world transform the city with murals and installations inspired by local culture. In the link below you can find all the murals in Estarreja:
https://www.cm-estarreja.pt/estau
Alternative road parallel to EN 109 that offers a few kilometers quieter. Furthermore, it runs through corn fields, making the landscape even more picturesque.
